MainSubtitle: Manage and switch between Claude Code accounts. Double-click a row to switch.
TrayRunningBody: Running in the system tray. Click to open.
RunningSwitchWarn:
A running claude session was detected. Switching may affect that session.
Continue?
SetShellDesc: Choose the shell used to launch claude in a new window.
SetStatusLine: Show account status line in Claude (concurrent runs)
MsgCaptureNoLogin: No Claude account is currently signed in. Sign in with 'claude' in a terminal first.
MsgCaptureNamePromptEmail:
Currently signed in: {0}
Name for this profile:
MsgAddNamePrompt: Name of the account to add:
MsgAddInfo:
claude will start in a new window. Sign in there, then press Refresh.
(This profile is an isolated login that does not touch ~/.claude.)
MsgSwitchSelect: Select a profile to switch to.
MsgSwitchNeedLogin: This profile is not signed in yet. (sign-in required)
MsgSwitchAlready: This is already the active account.
MsgLaunchNeedLogin: This profile is not signed in yet. (sign-in required)
MsgLaunchFolderTitle: Choose a folder to run claude for '{0}'
MsgDeleteConfirm:
Delete the profile '{0}'? Its saved login will also be removed.
(The current ~/.claude login is not affected.)
SkipDlgRemember: Remember this choice and don't ask again
SkipDlgMessage:
Launching claude for the '{0}' account.

The --dangerously-skip-permissions flag skips permission prompts while running. It is convenient but can be risky, so use it only for work you trust.
UpdAvailable: A new version {0} is available. Update now?
